items.title: Fraaie comeback brengt Heerenveen zege op PEC Zwolle
items.summary: SC Heerenveen heeft dankzij een knappe comeback PEC Zwolle verslagen. De Friezen kwamen zondag razendsnel met 0-2 achter, maar nadat Maxence Rivera uit het niets de aansluitingstreffer maakte, nam de thuisclub het duel in handen, om die niet meer weg te geven.

items.title: VI Live: heerlijk Eredivisieslotstuk op Het Kasteel
items.summary: Zondag staan er vier wedstrijden in de Eredivisie op het programma. Het duel van Feyenoord volgen we in een aparte live. In deze VI Live blijf je op de hoogte van SC Heerenveen tegen PEC Zwolle, Telstar tegen FC Twente, Sparta Rotterdam tegen NEC én het belangrijkste buitenlandse voetbal.

items.title: Telstar loopt de grote beloning toch mis na sterke start tegen Twente
items.summary: FC Twente is zondagmiddag op bezoek bij Telstar ontsnapt aan een nederlaag. De degradatiekandidaat was dicht bij zijn tweede thuiszege van het seizoen in de Eredivisie, maar door toedoen van Marko Pjaca bleef die overwinning toch uit.

items.title: Leeds sink Birmingham in penalty shootout to reach FA Cup fifth round
items.summary: Leeds United advanced to the fifth roundof the FA Cup, with sights set on a possible first quarter-final place since 2002-03. But after scoring a last-gasp equaliser to take the tie to a penalty shootout, Birmingham City could draw some consolation from pushing their top-flight opponents. Sean Longstaff scored the clinching penalty, after Patrick Roberts, who had scored in the 89th minute to earn Birmingham parity, blasted his over the crossbar. Lucas Perri also saved from Tommy Doyle, who was probably man of the match. Continue reading...

items.title: Boel is furieus over arbitrage: 'Word je schijtziek van als team'
items.summary: Volgens Melvin Boel is Go Ahead Eagles flink benadeeld in de uitwedstrijd tegen Feyenoord. De trainer van de ploeg uit Deventer gaf na afloop direct aan dat de rode kaart voor Thibo Baeten in zijn ogen terecht was, maar hij was wel ontstemd over de strafschop die Feyenoord kreeg. De beslissing om Feyenoord een strafschop te geven, paste volgens Boel wel bij het algehele optreden van scheidsrechter Marc Nagtegaal.

items.title: Real Madrid 'lucky to have' Trent, says Arbeloa
items.summary: Alvaro Arbeloa praised Trent Alexander-Arnold, saying Real Madrid are "lucky to have" him after the full-back starred, providing an assist, in Real Madrid's 4-1 win over Real Sociedad.

items.title: Macclesfield's Rooney: 'Ambition' to manage higher
items.summary: John Rooney is clear in his ambition to manage at the highest level possible but he wants to go on that journey with Macclesfield.

items.title: Barça's Flick praises ref 'good communication'
items.summary: Barcelona coach Hansi Flick said his chat with the referee from Thursday's 4-0 defeat to Atlético Madrid is a secret, but praised the communication.

items.title: Leeds survive FA Cup scare, win penalty shootout
items.summary: Leeds United survived in the FA Cup to win a penalty shootout against Birmingham City on Sunday.

items.title: Reijnders warns Arsenal: Man City 'on the hunt'
items.summary: Tijjani Reijnders has warned Arsenal that Manchester City remain firmly "on the hunt" for the Premier League title.

items.title: Van Persie vertelt na frustrerend duel over Tengstedt als penaltynemer
items.summary: Jakub Moder was tegen Go Ahead Eagles de eerste penaltynemer van Feyenoord, Ayase Ueda de tweede en Inbeom Hwang de derde. Het trio stond op het veld toen Feyenoord een late strafschop kreeg, die bijzonder genoeg genomen werd door Casper Tengstedt. Trainer Robin van Persie gaf wat meer uitleg over deze situatie.

items.title: Chelsea 2-0 Liverpool, Aston Villa 3-7 Tottenham and more: WSL clockwatch – as it happened
items.summary: Spurs put seven past Aston Villa in 10-goal thriller while Lauren James stars as Chelsea beat Liverpool What a devastating counter attack from Everton! They have an overload going forward and work it to Hannah Blundell. Blundell plays the ball into the path of Honoka Hayashi who taps into an empty net. Well this is a surprise. London City Lionesses work it well down the right and deliver the ball into the box. Nikita Parris bravely meets it and heads the ball back across goal. What a start for the away side. Continue reading...

items.title: GB's Nightingale, Bankes win Winter Olympics gold in snowboard cross
items.summary: Great Britain have claimed a second gold medal at the 2026 Winter Olympics after Huw Nightingale and Charlotte Bankes won the mixed team snowboard cross event.

items.title: Pereira announced as Forest boss on 18-month deal
items.summary: Nottingham Forest appoint Vitor Pereira on an 18-month deal as the 57-year-old becomes the club's fourth manager of the campaign.

items.title: Pakistan chasing 176 to beat India at T20 World Cup LIVE!
European football: Serie A referees’ chief apologises after controversial Kalulu red card
items.summary: Juventus lost 3-2 in dramatic fashion away at Inter Spalletti and Chiellini confronted referee La Penna Serie A’s referee designator Gianluca Rocchi said match official Federico La Penna was “clearly wrong” in showing Juventus defender Pierre Kalulu a second yellow card during Saturday’s loss at Inter, and apologised over the incident. Kalulu was sent off after Inter’s Alessandro Bastoni tumbled to the ground and immediately gestured towards the referee demanding a card, indicating that Kalulu had grabbed his shirt to bring him down. Television footage suggested there was no contact between the players. Juventus, down to 10 men after the sending off, lost 3-2, meaning Inter are now eight points clear at the top. This story will be updated Continue reading...

items.title: Captain Van Dijk wants 'so important' Salah to stay
items.summary: Liverpool captain hails the importance of Mohamed Salah, why the Egyptian brings more than goals and his desire for Salah to stay beyond the summer

items.title: Nottingham Forest confirm Vítor Pereira as fourth head coach of season
items.summary: Sean Dyche was sacked after 25 games in the role Pereira starts with Europa League tie at Fenerbahce Nottingham Forest have confirmed the appointment of Vítor Pereira as their fourth head coach of the season. The former Wolves manager takes over from Sean Dyche, who was sacked on Thursday, on a contract until June 2027. Pereira inherits a team one place and three points above the relegation zone. Dyche lasted 25 matches after replacing Ange Postecoglou, who was given eight games as the successor to Nuno Espírito Santo. Continue reading...

items.title: Pereira confirmed as new Forest head coach
items.summary: Vitor Pereira has been confirmed as the new Nottingham Forest head coach on an 18-month deal.

items.title: Six Nations 2026 LIVE! France making lightning fast start against Wales
